[2018-10-15 14:38] drevoed: What's the eficiency of soaking xeno / urban sentry when they provide 1 threat? (for example when your main abosrber is Ematrix)
[2018-10-15 14:38] drevoed: is it still worse than walls, or becomes about even?
[2018-10-15 14:39] shadourow (Rando): Or... maybe not that obvious, indeed
[2018-10-15 14:41] shadourow (Rando): Imo, 1 threat is worth a bit less than 1gold each turn (more exactly (2*1.33 - 2 = 0.66 gold, the minimum inflation on an useless engi)
while a green is worth 1.33 gold, which, imo, basically means that you don't want that threat
[2018-10-15 14:43] shadourow (Rando): no, he wants to sac a current sentry
[2018-10-15 14:45] shadourow (Rando): That's obviously fine if you're out of wall supply, but I think the question is :

Is it better to build a sentry and to with a current sentry or to build a wall and soak with this wall ?

[2018-10-15 14:47] shadourow (Rando): (if you have a matrix)

[2018-10-16 02:59] willflame: if my opponent goes 3rd engi in a set without any good soak and the attackers are decent, what determines whether i mirror or rush?
[2018-10-16 03:00] willflame: because i've played a few sets that don't look 3rd engi but my opponent goes high econ anyway, and my rush is ineffective
[2018-10-16 03:01] camzace: probably need specific examples
[2018-10-16 03:01] camzace: otherwise the answer will be 'it depends on the set'
[2018-10-16 03:02] willflame: hmm, how about relative strength of rushes then?
[2018-10-16 03:02] willflame: like is Tarsier rush faster/stronger than Militia rush?
[2018-10-16 03:02] camzace: militia should definitely not influence whether you rush or not
[2018-10-16 03:03] mrguy888: you never mentioned absorb
[2018-10-16 03:03] camzace: i guess there's a tiny chance it does, but probably not
[2018-10-16 03:03] mrguy888: absorb determines what you do more than attackers or soak
[2018-10-16 03:03] willflame: right, so how much better absorb is Doomed Wall compared to Wall
[2018-10-16 03:04] willflame: because i think i underestimate Doomed Wall often
[2018-10-16 03:04] camzace: its also good soak at the same time
[2018-10-16 03:04] camzace: well reasonable soak
[2018-10-16 03:06] amalloy: i think whether opp gets 3rd engi rarely influences whether you should
[2018-10-16 03:06] amalloy: if 3rd engi is good, you get it whether they do or not; if it's bad, you shouldn't get it
[2018-10-16 03:07] willflame: ok but if it's bad and they get a 3rd engi, should i rush or play normally?
[2018-10-16 03:07] mrguy888: doomed wall is quite good soak
[2018-10-16 03:07] camzace: if its bad then it is punishable by an aggressive strategy
[2018-10-16 03:07] camzace: the set dictates whether the 3rd engi is bad or not
[2018-10-16 03:07] mrguy888: if its bad third engi rush and play normally is the same thing right?
[2018-10-16 03:07] camzace: and that includes whether there are aggressive enough units
[2018-10-16 03:08] camzace: do whichever wins you more games, whether that's rush or mirror
[2018-10-16 03:08] willflame: would p2 cut a drone to get Fastimus instead of Natural Conduit?
[2018-10-16 03:08] camzace: if the set supports it sure
[2018-10-16 03:08] mrguy888: well that depends on if you want red or green
[2018-10-16 03:09] willflame: you want both eventually (Tantalum Ray, Feral Warden) but the random set has, say Electrovore
[2018-10-16 03:09] amalloy: i feel like the question is more like "should i go mid econ or low econ", not "normal or rush"
[2018-10-16 03:09] amalloy: which is still not easily answerable but at least it makes sense
[2018-10-16 03:09] camzace: rush and low econ seem pretty synonymous to me
[2018-10-16 03:09] willflame: yeah that's probably a better way to word it ^
[2018-10-16 03:10] mrguy888: tantalum doesnt seem like a unit you want in that set of units
[2018-10-16 03:10] mrguy888: feral you get when you need your absorber
[2018-10-16 03:10] amalloy: yeah, in base plus those there i buy no rays
[2018-10-16 03:10] camzace: if your opp goes p1 3rd engi in base + that set
[2018-10-16 03:10] camzace: he definitely deserves to lose
[2018-10-16 03:11] willflame: what if there's Scorchilla
[2018-10-16 03:12] willflame: or do you not buy Scorchilla in base either
[2018-10-16 03:13] willflame: i just feel like going low econ can't sustain the attack well against a high econ with a better absorber than Wall
[2018-10-16 03:13] camzace: well maybe it can't depending on the absorber and the set
[2018-10-16 03:14] mrguy888: if low econ cant beat it then why do you say the third engi is bad?
[2018-10-16 03:15] willflame: probably because i'm bad at reading sets
[2018-10-16 03:16] willflame: and 3rd engi seems somewhat reasonable but isn't what i would have done in their place
[2018-10-16 03:16] amalloy: @WillFlame what mrguy is saying is that you are saying contradictory things. if 3rd engi is bad, it's because you think some kind of rush beats it. you can't *also* say that the rush can't sustain, in the same set: if that's true, 3rd engi was good
[2018-10-16 03:17] amalloy: you can be wrong about which one is true (that's reading the set wrong), but you can't think they're both true
[2018-10-16 03:17] mrguy888: this is where i am getting confused yes
[2018-10-16 03:17] willflame: yeah, i see that. sorry
[2018-10-16 03:17] mrguy888: without a set i cant really answer your question of which one is true
[2018-10-16 03:17] camzace: that was what i was trying to get at too
[2018-10-16 03:18] willflame: i *think* 3rd engi is bad in a set, so i play the rush i had envisioned against it. but i lose

[2018-10-22 21:22] amalloy: and if you do win with strategy X, then you've also learned a lot! you've found a strategy that is at least somewhat playable, and you've shown that your fundamentals are decent enough to execute it passably well

[2018-10-23 00:02] parchmentengineer: I think I'm understanding this more.
[2018-10-23 00:03] parchmentengineer: You both pursue defense, keeping it exactly one above the opponent's attack potential, while steadily ramping up your offense.
[2018-10-23 00:03] parchmentengineer: Eventually, one player won't be able to keep their defense up any longer, and that player will get breached and lose.
[2018-10-23 00:04] amalloy: that's a reasonable summary of a game where both players are following the standard style
[2018-10-23 00:05] willflame: the goal of a standard game is to ramp up offense faster so your opponent has to spend gold on defence and not on attack, and then you can spend more on attack, etc
[2018-10-23 00:05] willflame: which is why i find it pretty hard to pull out of a losing game
[2018-10-23 00:08] swapgo: Yeah sort of, unless the opponent's attack output is dwindling in some way (Tantalum Health, Steelsplitters for Odin), the moment you can't afford to add attack and aren't close to breaching them is the moment you've lost
[2018-10-23 00:09] parchmentengineer: Alright. Always add attack, always keep up the pressure.
[2018-10-23 00:09] parchmentengineer: I'm going against the Expert Bot now. Wish me luck.
[2018-10-23 00:10] swapgo: The correct priority is "Figure out how many drones you will need to sustain attack and some marginal defense, then start cranking out attack"

[2018-10-26 17:59] sfayne: I got a question regarding Arka or absorber that can click to attack
[2018-10-26 18:00] sfayne: Arka has the same attack/absorb difference as wall (attack for 4 and absorb 6 or attack for 0 and absorb 2)
[2018-10-26 18:01] sfayne: So my understanding is that absorbing on Arka or Wall is technically as valuable, except that Arka has more defense tempo
[2018-10-26 18:01] sfayne: If Arka kills something useful and you can click it without having too much trouble clicking it, you should
[2018-10-26 18:03] sfayne: now then, what if Arka doesn't kill anything particularly useful, but it's not too hard to click it?
[2018-10-26 18:03] sfayne: What are the factors that determine if it's worth doing or not?
[2018-10-26 18:04] sfayne: I know supply would be a factor
[2018-10-26 18:04] sfayne: I assume the aggressor has more reasons to click it than the aggressed
[2018-10-26 18:06] sfayne: But if it's a mirror situation and I can spend my resources for either defensive or offensive options of equal value, should I click or hold Arka?
[2018-10-26 18:08] sfayne: Also does the fact that I'm half a turn ahead play a role of some sort in the way I should estimate resources? (that inflation stuff)
[2018-10-26 18:09] sfayne: If either of the click or hold option would overdefend by one, the other one will be better in most cases, is that correct?
[2018-10-26 18:10] amalloy: @Sfayne https://youtu.be/2juznN4iBZ4?t=362 discusses the value of clicking arka
Arka Sodara - Prismata Unit Analysis
If you're interested in hanging out and asking questions while I play Prismata, check out my twitch channel: https://www.twitch.tv/msven Follow me on Twitter...
[2018-10-26 18:10] sfayne: Ah I haven't watched that one yet
[2018-10-26 18:11] sfayne: I mean it's the same for Doomed mech, Omega etc... but I assume the explanation works for those too
[2018-10-26 18:11] sfayne: I'll watch it later, thanks
[2018-10-26 18:11] amalloy: the tldw is that buying defenders sucks
[2018-10-26 18:12] amalloy: so if clicking arka makes you buy defenders instead of attackers it's bad
[2018-10-26 18:19] mrguy888: search for "holding omega" by me for my explanation of it.
